response.justification: Candidate is a strong generalist software craftsman with broad full-stack experience and genuine tooling/IDE-adjacent work (LabWindows/CVI at NI, DLang test runner, MCP code generators), but the role specifically requires deep Java/Kotlin/JVM and Spring ecosystem expertise. The resume lists Java only as a peripheral skill with no recent Spring, Kotlin, or JVM-internals experience, and the bulk of recent work is in TypeScript/Node and DLang. Misaligned on the core required stack.
strengths: Genuine interest and experience in tooling (IDE component work at NI, custom test runner, MCP generators)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, workshops on testing
Proven on complex, large-scale systems (low-latency micro-services at Sociomantic handling millions of req/s)
concerns: No demonstrated Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience
Java listed only as a skill keyword; no Kotlin or deep JVM-internals signal in employment history
Recent stack is TypeScript/Node/DLang, not JVM — would require significant ramp-up
